title: Hermes's Mix-Up
tagline: Hermes delivered every message this week. To the wrong people. He was very fast about it.

characters.guest: Hermes — fastest messenger god, zero patience for reading addresses carefully. He delivered Zeus's invitation to Perseus's adventure club to Baby Hercules (who showed up ready to destroy things). He delivered Athena's book order to Zeus (who opened it, understood nothing, and tried to use the thunderbolt diagrams as actual plans). He delivered Achilles's race schedule to Poseidon (who sent dolphins as spectators to the wrong event). He means well. He is just very, very fast.
characters.focus: The group — dealing with the fallout of every message going to the wrong recipient

scenes.title: The Wrong Invitation
scenes.narration: Monday began with a mystery: Baby Hercules arrived at the Olympus Scholars' Reading Club meeting wearing his best lion cape and ready for adventure. The Scholars' Reading Club was Athena's quiet book group. It met in the library. It had a rule about not breaking furniture. Hercules looked at the rule. He looked at the furniture. Athena looked at Hermes's note attached to the invitation. The address said: PERSEUS JACKSON, ADVENTURE SOCIETY. Hermes had delivered it to the closest H.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena's quiet library reading group — small chairs, books everywhere — with Baby Hercules sitting in the middle of it in his best lion cape looking extremely ready for an adventure that is not happening here. Athena holds Hermes's delivery note. The address says PERSEUS JACKSON crossed out and rewritten as H next to it. Hermes visible as a blur in the doorway, already gone. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Cascade
scenes.narration: By midday they discovered the full scope. Zeus had received Athena's twelve-volume Advanced Philosophy of Architecture textbook and had used volume three as a thunderbolt targeting manual. It was not a thunderbolt targeting manual. He had aimed three bolts at angles based on load-bearing diagrams. One pillar on the east terrace was now structurally improved. The other two bolts were, technically, somewhere.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Zeus proudly showing Athena an open textbook — Architecture volume 3, loaded with columns and beam diagrams — which he's annotated with thunderbolt trajectory arrows. Athena looks at it with the specific expression of someone whose book has been fundamentally misused. In the background, one Olympus pillar looks notably improved. Two others have scorch marks at interesting angles. 16:9.

scenes.title: The Race Dolphins
scenes.narration: Achilles's race schedule had gone to Poseidon, who had sent six dolphins as spectators to Athena's reading club (which was now also a furniture-integrity situation with Hercules). The dolphins were in the library. Athena was taking deep breaths. The dolphins were very enthusiastic readers. Archie looked at the camera with the expression of an owl who had seen enough.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Six dolphins somehow in Athena's library, looking at books with their noses, very enthusiastic. Baby Hercules is delighted and trying to read to one of them. Athena standing in the middle of this taking very deliberate deep breaths. Archie on a high shelf looking directly at the camera. 16:9.

scenes.title: Finding Hermes
scenes.narration: They found Hermes at the message sorting station — he had delivered four hundred and twelve messages that day, a personal record. He was very pleased. He said: 'All delivered!' They showed him the evidence: Hercules at the reading club, Zeus's architecture-thunderbolt experiment, dolphins in the library. Hermes looked at each. He said: 'I delivered them to the right addresses.' Athena said: 'You delivered them to the wrong people.' He looked confused. He genuinely didn't understand the difference.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Hermes at his sorting station — very fast, very pleased with himself, four hundred message receipts on the wall. The group standing before him presenting evidence: photo of library dolphins, photo of Zeus's angle diagrams, Hercules standing behind them giving a wave. Hermes looking at all this genuinely confused about what went wrong. 16:9.

scenes.title: Athena's New System
scenes.narration: Athena had Hermes sit down. She explained the difference between address and recipient. Hermes said: 'But the address was close enough.' She said: 'Messages are for the person named, not the nearest person.' Hermes thought about this for a long time — three full seconds, which for Hermes was geological. He said: 'That is a very different system than I've been using.' He agreed to try it.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Athena sitting Hermes down — he's sitting for the first time we've seen him sit, looking slightly uncomfortable at the pace. She explains with a diagram: name = recipient. He thinks for three seconds (for him, enormous). His face: genuinely recalibrating. Archie watches. 16:9.

scenes.title: Re-delivery
scenes.narration: Hermes re-delivered everything correctly. It took six more minutes, which he found agonizing. The dolphins returned to the Olympus Sea. Hercules found the actual adventure club (Perseus was thrilled). Zeus returned volume three of the architecture textbook. He had, in fairness, also flagged three genuine structural improvements in the margins. Athena kept his notes.
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Happy resolution montage — dolphins back in the Olympus Sea waving fins, Hercules arriving at a proper adventure meeting with Perseus looking delighted, Zeus returning the textbook while Athena notices his (surprisingly good) structural notes in the margins, Hermes delivering one message very carefully and slowly for him, checking the name twice. 16:9.

scenes.title: What Did We Learn?
scenes.narration: Being fast is wonderful. Being fast at the wrong thing doesn't help anyone. The message matters more than the speed — if the right information gets to the wrong person, it's not really delivered at all. Hermes now checks the name twice. He still holds the all-time delivery record. See you next time on Little Olympus!
scenes.visual_prompt: Little Olympus kids cartoon style. Final card — Hermes checking a message name TWICE before zooming off, winged sandals sparkling. The rest of the group waving. Archie does not look at the camera this episode. He feels he has said enough. 16:9.

lesson: Speed matters less than accuracy. The fastest wrong answer is still wrong.
youtube_title: Hermes Delivered ALL the Wrong Messages! 📨😂 | Little Olympus Episode 11
next_episode_preview: Episode 12 — Perseus Visits Medusa Again! She said he could come back. She meant it. Probably.
